description_long: This is a double-ended open wrench made of metal, featuring two U-shaped jaws of different sizes at each end. The body is straight and flat, with a slight taper toward each jaw, and the metal surface shows a matte, slightly aged finish. Both jaws are fixed and open, designed for gripping and turning nuts or bolts of corresponding sizes. The wrench appears to be forged from a single piece of metal, likely steel, and has no visible rubber or plastic components. There are no visible brand markings or decorative elements, and the handle is smooth without any grip pattern. The surface shows some minor discoloration and wear, suggesting regular use but no significant rust or damage.
description: A metal double-ended open wrench with two different-sized U-shaped jaws and a smooth, slightly worn surface.
description_view_0: The wrench is seen from the side, clearly showing both open jaws and the straight, flat handle.
description_view_1: A narrow edge-on view, revealing the thin profile and flatness of the wrench.
description_view_2: The opposite side view, again displaying both open jaws and the full length of the handle.
description_view_3: Another edge-on view, similar to view 1, emphasizing the slim, solid construction.
attribute_rust_or_wear_marks: Minor wear and discoloration visible on the surface, especially near the jaws, but no significant rust.
